sources.supports: Node FileHandle.sync and promise-based filesystem primitives used by the adapter
file synchronization does not by itself guarantee persistence of the containing directory entry

finding: A failure after atomic rename but during parent-directory synchronization is not safely classifiable as applied or not-applied. The caller must classify it as indeterminate, reread durable state, accept only an exact terminal match, reject a conflicting terminal, and avoid a blind retry when no terminal can be confirmed.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical candidate files before commit
assertions: post-rename sync failure reconciles an exact persisted terminal
a conflicting persisted terminal is rejected without a second write
an unresolved indeterminate result fails closed without a second write

uncertainty: No physical power-loss or kernel crash was injected.
Directory fsync behavior varies by operating system and filesystem.
The adapter remains a same-host coordination mechanism.
A successful reconciliation confirms journal state, not peer work execution.
falsification_route: Inject failures after rename and before or during directory sync. Falsify the design if any path performs a second compare-and-set before rereading, accepts a nonidentical terminal, produces malformed journal JSON, or reports not-applied when the exact terminal is already readable.
